skill.name: senior-architect
skill.description: Software architecture guidance for planning scalable systems, comparing technology choices, and running simple local architecture helper scripts.
skill.summary: Plan software architecture decisions with reference guides and local helper scripts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code fences, generic headings, and placeholder text. The confirmed risks are legitimate but relevant: runnable Python scripts, user-selected local file output, command-heavy documentation, and a documented .env setup command. No evidence found of network access, credential exfiltration, dynamic code execution, malicious prompt injection, or real weak cryptography usage.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected Local File Write
medium_findings.description: The three Python scripts accept an output path and write JSON output with open(args.output, "w"). This is useful report behavior, but it can overwrite files accessible to the process if a user supplies an unsafe path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write calls are directly present and use a CLI-supplied output path. The behavior appears intended for reports, not malicious persistence or exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Operational Commands Documented for Agent Use
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands for Python scripts, package installation, Docker, Docker Compose, and kubectl. These are legitimate development commands, but an AI agent following them could modify dependencies, containers, or cluster resources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown command blocks and include tools with real side effects. They are documented examples, not automatically executed code.

low_findings.title: Environment File Setup Guidance
low_findings.description: The documentation includes a command to copy .env.example to .env. This does not read secrets, but it encourages creation of an environment file that may later hold sensitive values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env command is directly documented, but it copies a template rather than accessing or transmitting secrets. The risk depends on how users later populate the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis labeled Markdown fences and inline reference text as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Review found code fences, script examples, and reference-file mentions, not backtick execution in Ruby or another runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples or reference links, and no Ruby files or shell backtick operators are present. Some documented commands still carry operational risk and are handled separately.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but review found no hashlib, md5, sha1, crypto library calls, password hashing, or signature verification code at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no cryptographic implementation or weak hash API at the static locations. The matches appear caused by generic prose or substring artifacts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance at headings and list text. Review found generic anti-pattern and reference documentation sections, not host enumeration, credential discovery, or network scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation headings or placeholder explanatory text. No commands or code paths enumerate the host system beyond validating a user-supplied target path.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI Output Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts write reports to a caller-provided path when --json and --output are used. This can overwrite local files if invoked with an unsafe destination.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in each script and uses a user-controlled file path. There is no evidence that the write is hidden or malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: High-Impact Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes commands such as npm install, Docker Compose, and kubectl apply. These commands can change local or remote state when a user or agent runs them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and have known side effects. They are examples in documentation, so execution requires user or agent action outside the skill file itself.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear architecture decisions before they commit to complex technology choices. This skill provides structured guidance, reference patterns, and local helper scripts for architecture planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides system architecture planning across frontend, backend, mobile, database, DevOps, and cloud topics.
Provides reference documents for architecture patterns, system design workflows, and technology decisions.
Documents commands for local architecture helper scripts and common development workflows.
Includes three Python CLI scripts that validate a target path and print simple status reports.
Supports optional JSON report output from the included scripts.
limitations: Reference documents are broad templates and do not contain deep framework-specific implementation guidance.
The included scripts are lightweight placeholders and do not perform real dependency or architecture analysis.
The architecture diagram generator does not create visual diagrams in its current form.
Commands in the documentation can change local or deployment state if run without review.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Create a first-pass architecture outline, identify major components, and choose a practical technology stack.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing Codebase Direction
use_cases.description: Use the workflow references to frame maintainability, scalability, security, and performance trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Decision Records
use_cases.description: Compare options and document reasons for selecting frontend, backend, database, mobile, or cloud technologies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-architect skill to outline a simple architecture for [product]. Include main services, data stores, client apps, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Technology Stack Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tech decision guide to compare [option A], [option B], and [option C] for [project context]. Explain trade-offs and recommend one option.

prompt_templates.title: System Design Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this architecture proposal using the system design workflow. Focus on scalability, reliability, security, observability, and operational complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a phased migration from [current architecture] to [target architecture]. Include dependencies, decision points, rollback plans, and measurable success criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan an architecture for a mobile marketplace with real-time messaging and payments.
output_examples.output: A component map covering mobile clients, API services, messaging infrastructure, payment boundaries, PostgreSQL storage, authentication, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Compare Next.js, React Native, and Flutter for a customer-facing product.
output_examples.output: A decision summary with product fit, team skills, delivery speed, performance expectations, platform coverage, and a recommended direction.

output_examples.input: Review whether our project needs GraphQL or REST APIs.
output_examples.output: A trade-off review covering client data needs, caching, schema governance, operational tooling, developer experience, and migration cost.

best_practices: Ask for business goals, scale expectations, team skills, and operating constraints before choosing technologies.
Treat generated architecture recommendations as drafts that need review by project owners and security teams.
Review commands before execution, especially Docker, kubectl, package installation, and file output commands.
anti_patterns: Do not run deployment commands from the documentation against production without explicit approval.
Do not assume the included scripts perform deep code analysis or dependency inspection.
Do not accept a technology recommendation without comparing maintainability, security, cost, and team capability.

faq.question: Does this skill generate real architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: No. The included diagram script prints a simple report and does not create visual diagram files.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze my full dependency graph?
faq.answer: Not in its current form. The dependency analyzer script validates a target path and returns placeholder results.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a medium-risk warning because it includes runnable scripts and operational command examples.

faq.question: Does the skill access the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network requests in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should review package installation, Docker, kubectl, .env setup, and output file paths before execution.
